topic: Customs in International Context - International Trade
course_outcomes: Define international trade and key related terms
Identify players involved in international trade transactions
Outline the main steps in international trade transactions and the documents used
Explain methods of payment, modes of transport, Incoterms, and selected trade agreements

units.title: What International Trade Is
units.unit_goal: Understand the meaning of international trade and its most important core terms.

lessons.title: Meaning of International Trade
lessons.goal: Understand what international trade means.
micro_steps: Learn the meaning of international trade
Connect trade to goods, services, and capital
Explain why it is called a pillar of the global economy

lessons.title: Import, Export, and Trade Balance
lessons.goal: Understand imports, exports, and trade balance.
micro_steps: Differentiate trade surplus and trade deficit

lessons.title: Trade Terms Match
lessons.goal: Match basic trade terms to their meanings.
micro_steps: Match HS Code to product classification
Match tariff to tax on imports or exports
Match trade finance to financing trade transactions
Match dumping to below-home-market foreign pricing

lessons.title: Starter Quiz
lessons.goal: Test your understanding of the basic language of international trade.
micro_steps: Identify the right definition of trade terms

lessons.title: Unit 1 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you can define international trade and the core terms that support it.
micro_steps: Define import, export, and trade balance
Explain at least four common trade terms

units.title: Why Countries Trade
units.unit_goal: Understand why international trade occurs and what benefits and problems it creates.

lessons.title: Why Trade Happens
lessons.goal: Understand the main causes of international trade.
micro_steps: Explain comparative advantage in simple terms
Explain how government taxes and subsidies can create trade advantages

lessons.title: Advantages of Trade
lessons.goal: Understand the major benefits of international trade.
micro_steps: Explain how trade creates monetary gains
Explain specialization and economies of scale
Explain competition and product quality improvement
Explain technology transfer and employment effects

lessons.title: Disadvantages of Trade
lessons.goal: Understand the major risks and costs of international trade.
micro_steps: Explain dangers to infant industries and local producers
Explain environmental, labor, and deficit-related concerns

lessons.title: Benefit or Risk?
lessons.goal: Sort trade outcomes into advantages and disadvantages.
micro_steps: Match trade deficit strain to risk

lessons.title: Unit 2 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Prove you understand why countries trade and what trade can do well or badly.

units.title: Types of Trade and Trade Structure
units.unit_goal: Understand the main types and classifications of international trade.

lessons.title: Import, Export, and Re-export
lessons.goal: Understand the three basic trade forms.

lessons.title: Bilateral and Multilateral Trade
lessons.goal: Understand classification by number of countries.
micro_steps: Compare agreements between two countries and broader systems

lessons.title: Trade Form Match
lessons.goal: Match trade examples to the right trade type.
micro_steps: Match Kenya importing vehicles to import trade
Match Kenya exporting tea to export trade
Match Dubai re-export example to entrepôt trade
Match UK-Kenya agreement to bilateral trade

lessons.title: Unit 3 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you can classify trade correctly.

units.title: Players in International Trade
units.unit_goal: Identify all major players in an international trade transaction and understand their roles.

lessons.title: Core Commercial Players
lessons.goal: Understand the seller, buyer, and their agents.
micro_steps: Differentiate selling agent and buying agent

lessons.title: Transport and Customs Players
lessons.goal: Understand the logistics actors in international trade.
micro_steps: Explain the role of the overseas shipping or airline agent
Explain the role of the shipping agent in the importing country

lessons.title: Banks and Consolidators
lessons.goal: Understand support roles in international trade.
micro_steps: Explain the role of a consolidator
Connect these roles to financing and grouped shipment handling

lessons.title: Player Role Match
lessons.goal: Match each player to the correct role.
micro_steps: Match customs administration to compliance and duty collection
Match banks to finance and payment support
Match carrier to movement of goods

lessons.title: Unit 4 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate that you know the players and their functions.
micro_steps: Connect each role to the trade transaction process

units.title: Steps in International Trade
units.unit_goal: Understand the main stages in an international trade transaction.

lessons.title: Order and Confirmation
lessons.goal: Understand the early commercial stages of a trade transaction.

lessons.title: Shipment and Payment
lessons.goal: Understand the transport and settlement stages.

lessons.title: Customs Clearance Reminder
lessons.goal: Understand that customs clearance is broader than tax payment.
micro_steps: Separate payment of duties from administrative compliance
Connect customs clearance to the wider process

lessons.title: Transaction Flow Match
lessons.goal: Put the trade transaction in the right order.

lessons.title: Unit 5 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Prove you can explain the steps of an international trade transaction.
micro_steps: List the main transaction steps in order
Explain what happens at each step
Name example documents at each step

units.title: Documents Used in International Trade
units.unit_goal: Master the main document categories and important individual documents used in trade.

lessons.title: Document Categories
lessons.goal: Understand the five main categories of trade documents.
micro_steps: Match each category to its function
Recognize why different trades need different document mixes

lessons.title: Commercial Documents
lessons.goal: Understand requisition orders, purchase orders, contracts of sale, proforma invoices, commercial invoices, consular invoices, and packing lists.
micro_steps: Differentiate requisition order from purchase order
Differentiate proforma invoice from commercial invoice
Explain consular invoice and packing list

lessons.title: Transport Documents
lessons.goal: Understand the main transport documents.
micro_steps: Explain the bill of lading and its three functions
Differentiate original bill of lading and seaway bill
Explain airway bill and why it is not a document of title
Explain cargo manifest, delivery order, and road/rail documents

lessons.title: Document Role Match
lessons.goal: Match individual documents to their legal or practical function.
micro_steps: Match commercial invoice to customs valuation
Match packing list to cargo itemization
Match bill of lading to receipt, contract, and title
Match airway bill to movement certificate

lessons.title: Document Scenario Lab
lessons.goal: Apply the right documents to realistic trade situations.
micro_steps: Choose the right document for customs valuation
Choose the right document for title control in sea freight
Choose the right document for grouped air cargo

lessons.title: Unit 6 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate strong mastery of international trade documentation.
micro_steps: Explain major commercial and transport documents
Explain why the right document matters in customs and logistics

units.title: Methods of Payment in International Trade
units.unit_goal: Understand the major payment methods and the risks they create for exporter and importer.

lessons.title: Cash in Advance and Letters of Credit
lessons.goal: Understand two major payment methods with very different risk profiles.
micro_steps: Identify its risk for buyer and exporter
Explain how an LC protects both sides differently

lessons.title: Documentary Collections
lessons.goal: Understand documentary collections and their two forms.
micro_steps: Explain why title control differs across sea and air freight

lessons.title: Open Account and Consignment
lessons.goal: Understand exporter-risk-heavy payment arrangements.
micro_steps: Explain why it favors the importer
Explain why consignment is risky for the exporter

lessons.title: Risk Ladder Match
lessons.goal: Arrange payment methods from safer to riskier for exporter and importer.
micro_steps: Match cash-in-advance to high importer risk
Match open account to high exporter risk
Match LC to stronger bank-backed security

lessons.title: Unit 7 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you understand trade payment methods and the risk logic behind them.
micro_steps: Explain when each method is likely to be used

units.title: Modes of Transport and Logistics Choice
units.unit_goal: Understand transport modes and how to choose among them.

lessons.title: Main Transport Modes
lessons.goal: Identify the main transport modes listed in the presentation.
micro_steps: Recognize that more than one mode is often used
Connect transport mode to cargo needs

lessons.title: Choosing the Right Mode
lessons.goal: Understand the practical criteria used in transport selection.

lessons.title: Intermodal and Development Impact
lessons.goal: Understand integration and broader economic value of transport systems.
micro_steps: Explain why maritime transport matters globally
Explain how roads, ports, and railways support local development

lessons.title: Mode Selection Challenge
lessons.goal: Pick the most suitable mode for different goods and destinations.
micro_steps: Choose a mode for urgent cargo
Choose a mode for bulk long-distance cargo
Choose a mode for specialized liquid or gas cargo

lessons.title: Unit 8 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate that you can explain and choose transport modes intelligently.
micro_steps: Apply mode choice to business scenarios

units.title: Incoterms 2020
units.unit_goal: Understand what Incoterms do, what they do not do, and how the 11 rules are organized.

lessons.title: Purpose of Incoterms
lessons.goal: Understand why Incoterms were created and what they solve.

lessons.title: What Incoterms Cover and Do Not Cover
lessons.goal: Understand the boundaries of Incoterms.
micro_steps: List the things Incoterms do not cover
Differentiate risk transfer from ownership transfer

lessons.title: Key Changes in Incoterms 2020
lessons.goal: Understand the major updates from older versions.
micro_steps: Explain FCA onboard bill of lading change
Explain CIP versus CIF insurance changes

lessons.title: 11 Rules Map
lessons.goal: Sort the 11 Incoterms into the correct categories.
micro_steps: Group EXW to DDP under any mode(s)
Group FAS to CIF under sea/inland waterway
Recognize why sea rules differ from multimodal rules

lessons.title: Unit 9 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you understand the structure and purpose of Incoterms 2020.
micro_steps: Explain what they cover and do not cover

units.title: The 11 Incoterms in Practice
units.unit_goal: Understand the meaning and basic seller-buyer responsibility split under each Incoterm.

lessons.title: Any-Mode Rules: EXW to CIP
lessons.goal: Understand EXW, FCA, CPT, and CIP.
micro_steps: Explain CIP and added insurance duty

lessons.title: Any-Mode Rules: DAP to DDP
lessons.goal: Understand DAP, DPU, and DDP.
micro_steps: Explain DDP and maximum seller responsibility

lessons.title: Sea Rules: FAS to CIF
lessons.goal: Understand FAS, FOB, CFR, and CIF.

lessons.title: Incoterm Scenario Lab
lessons.goal: Choose the right term for practical trade situations.
micro_steps: Pick the term where seller bears maximum risk
Pick the term where seller unloads at destination
Pick a sea-only rule where seller pays freight and insurance

lessons.title: Unit 10 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate rule-by-rule understanding of Incoterms 2020.
micro_steps: Apply them to shipping and risk scenarios

units.title: Kenya-EU and Kenya-UK EPAs
units.unit_goal: Understand the two EPAs, their objectives, implementation, challenges, and mitigation measures.

lessons.title: Kenya-EU EPA Basics
lessons.goal: Understand the purpose and broad structure of the Kenya-EU EPA.

lessons.title: Kenya-EU EPA Implementation
lessons.goal: Understand duty treatment and phasedown structure under the Kenya-EU EPA.
micro_steps: Explain treatment of Kenyan exports to the EU
Explain duty-free treatment in Annex II(a)
Explain non-liberalized treatment under Annex II(d)

lessons.title: Kenya-UK EPA Basics and Implementation
lessons.goal: Understand the Kenya-UK EPA and how it is implemented.
micro_steps: Explain Annex II(a) to II(d) structure
Explain UK duty-free access for Kenyan goods

lessons.title: EPA Challenges and Mitigation
lessons.goal: Link EPA risks to proposed solutions.
micro_steps: Match revenue loss to domestic tax reform
Match local competition pressure to industrial upgrading
Match EAC coordination challenge to broader ratification

lessons.title: Unit 11 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you understand the EPAs as practical trade policy instruments.
micro_steps: Explain the purpose of each EPA
Explain major challenges and mitigation measures

units.title: Grand Mastery Path
units.unit_goal: Bring the full topic together from terms to transaction flow, documents, payments, logistics, Incoterms, and EPAs.

lessons.title: Mixed Process Quiz
lessons.goal: Review the entire trade transaction process from beginning to end.
micro_steps: Match documents to the correct stage

lessons.title: Risk and Responsibility Quiz
lessons.goal: Review payment methods, transport decisions, and Incoterms.

lessons.title: Integrated Trade Scenario Lab
lessons.goal: Handle a realistic international trade case using everything learned.
micro_steps: Identify the right players and documents

lessons.title: Grand Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate full mastery of customs in international context.
micro_steps: Define and explain the major trade terms
Explain the transaction steps and documents
Explain payment methods and transport choices
Explain Incoterms and the two EPAs

lessons.title: Legendary Review Path
lessons.goal: Final mixed review for long-term retention.

daily_practice.description: Recycles weak lessons, commonly confused documents, similar payment methods, and similar Incoterms.
